reasoning: The cash assets series (bank reserves) has been in a steep downward trend since mid-2025 (falling from ~3400 in July to ~2890 in Nov) due to quantitative tightening and liquidity dynamics. Given the negative drift, near-term increases are unlikely, though short-term variance provides some chance of a weekly bounce (P ~ 0.25 for Nov 16). For more distant dates, while the negative drift theoretically compounds, real-world uncertainty strongly increases because the Federal Reserve could alter policy, halt QT, or inject liquidity. Following the directive that uncertainty increases with horizon, the probability shifts toward 0.5 for December and February.
